objectives: Analyze performance characteristics of mx20022 library
Identify hot-path allocations, regex caching, cloning patterns
important_facts: Pattern validation uses compile-time codegen (no regex at runtime for newtypes)
FedNow UETR regex uses OnceLock correctly for caching
XML scanning uses string search, not full XML parsing (intentional design)
Benchmark coverage exists for parse, validate, and translate crates
Generated types use inline byte-level checks, no regex dependency in model crate
risks_issues: xml_scan format! allocations on every extract_element call: medium impact
Vec Validatable blanket impl allocates format! per index: low-medium impact on large messages
Translation pipeline clones strings that could borrow: low impact (translation is not hot-path)
Generated .to_string() on pattern violation messages: only on error path, acceptable

iteration.summary: Completed full performance analysis of mx20022. Found that the architecture is well-designed from a performance standpoint: codegen emits inline byte-level pattern checks avoiding runtime regex, FedNow UETR regex uses OnceLock caching, parse/serialize delegates to quick-xml+serde. Main findings: xml_scan functions allocate format strings per call, Vec validation allocates per-index format strings, and the translation pipeline does unnecessary string cloning. Benchmark coverage is solid across all three key crates.
